sutta: is quite an important time once when the exalted 1 was dwelling near savathi at jeter grove in anatap indica the householder anata pindika visited him and after saluting sat down at 1 side and the exalted 1 addressed anathapindipadas his arms given in your family household and he said yes lord but it consists of a coarse mess of broken rice grains together with salt rule i stopped here for a moment uh according to the commentary the buddha was asking anatha pindika the biggest supporter of the buddha about whether he gives arms to not to monks but to the world at nasa whether to beggars or whatever and the buddha continued householder whether 1 gives cause arms or choice arms if 1 gives unattentively without respect not with 1's own hand give what is to be thrown away and with a view or expectation in the future then wheresoever that arms giving bad fruit his mind will not turn to the enjoyment of excellent food a fine clothing of rich carriages to the enjoyment of the excellencies of the 5 senses and 1 sons and ones daughters 1 slaves messengers and workforce will have no desire to listen to 1 nor lend an ear nor bring understanding to bear on what 1 says and why such is the result household or deeds done casually but whether 1 give cause arms or choice arms householder if 1 give attentively respectfully shitty kattawa with 1's own hand gift other than what is to be thrown away and without a view or expectation in the future then wheresoever that arms giving bear fruit his mind will turn to the enjoyment enjoyment of good food of fine clothing of rich carriages of the excellencies of the 5 senses and 1 sons and ones daughters 1 slaves messengers and workforce will desire to listen to 1 will lend an ear and bring understanding to bear on what 1 says and why such is the result the householder of deeds done considerably i just stopped here to commend you'll hear the buddha is saying that if 1 gives 1 should give attentive leader and respectfully not um like some people if they give something to a beggar they throw it without respect and that's not good with 1's own hand if 1 is able to it is best that 1 does the giving oneself and give other than what is to be thrown away that means give not something that is too old or something that you don't value at all and you should also give without an expectation in the future without thinking of the reward then if then with that kind of giving then the blessings will be much more and the buddha continued long ago a householder there lived a brahmana called veilama he gave very rich gifts such as these he gave 84 000 golden balls filled with silver he gave 84 000 silver balls filled with gold he gave 84 000 copper bowls filled with treasure he gave 84 000 steeds the trappings of gold with banners of coal covered with nets of gold red he gave 84 000 chariots spread with lion skins tiger skins leopards skins saffron colored blankets with golden trappings golden banners covered with nets of gold thread he gave 84 000 milk cattle the tethers of fine jute with milk pails of silver he gave 84 000 maidens a dawn with jeweled earrings he gave 84 000 couches spread with fleecy covers white blankets and woodland woolen flower embroidered coverlets covered with drugs of antelope skins with awnings above had crimson cushions at each end he gave 80 4 thousand lengths of cloth a finest flecks of finest silk a finest wool of finest cotton and who shall tell of the food and the drink that he gave food both hard and soft sweet meats and syrups they float it seems like rivers perhaps householder you may think thus maybe the brahmin who made that very rich gift for someone else but thing not so for it was i who at that time was the brahmin it was i who made that very rich gift but when that arms was given householder there was no 1 worthy to receive the gift there was none to sanctify that gift although brahmin balama gave that very rich gift greater would have been the fruit thereof had he fed 1 person of right view i stop here for a moment so here the buddha is saying that long ago he was a brahmin by the name of bailama and he gave a lot of things away 84 000 golden bowls filled with silver 84 000 silver bows filled with gold 84 000 copper balls filled with treasure etc etc horses and chariots and clothings and slaves and cattle etc this figure 84 000 does not exactly mean 84 000 but it signifies a very great number in that range of 84 000. so the buddha is saying although he gave so much away yet there was no 1 worthy to receive the gift all the people at that time were putting as ordinary people because there was no dhamma in the world there being no dhamma in the world the people were ordinary people so the buddha said in spite of all that gifts that he gave so much greater would have been the married or the blessings that he fed 1 person with right view a person with right viewer is an arya that means here it means 1 with either was attain the first path or the first fruition why is it that he has given so much gives so much charity yet he says there was uh do it doesn't measure to 1 giving 1 person the right viewer and [Music] in fact this means that the blessings from all those gifts was not very very much and that is because ordinary people gutujanas they create more evil karma than good that is why the buddha said that most people are reborn in the woeful plains few come back to the human world or are reborn in the day of our realm [Music] but if 1 can give a gift to to an arya then the fruit would be very very much enough then the buddha continued though he gave that very rich gift although he fed that although he fed a 100 persons of right view that means otapanna greater would have been the proof that 1 1 sweetener sakadakamin a second fruition person though he gave that very rich gift although he fed a 100 once returners greater would have been the fruit thereof had he fed 1 non-returner anagamin third fruition person though he fed a 100 non-returners undergarments greater would have been the fruit thereof had he fed 1 arahant for fruition arya though he fed a 100 arahants creator would have been the fruit thereof had he fed 1 pacheco buddha though he fed a 100 pachika buddhas greater would have been the fruit thereof had he fed 1 tataka tata arahan though he fed the sangha of monks with the buddha at the head greater would have been the fruit thereof and he built a monastery for the use of the monks of the sangha of the surrounding country though he built a monastery greater would have been the fruit if he took refuge in the buddha dharma and sangha though with pious heart he took refuge in the buddha dharma and the sangha greater would have been the fruit thereof had he with by his heart and taken to keep the precepts abstention from taking life from taking what is not given from adultery from lying from intoxicating liquor the cause of sloth though with pie's heart he undertook to keep these precepts greater would have been the fruit thereof had had he made become a mere passing fragrance of meta that is good will or loving kindness though he may become just the fragrance of meta greater would have been the fruit thereof had he made become just for a finger snap the thought of impermanence and that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
so here uh the buddha continued that even if if he had fed 100 so tapana first fruition person the merit would have been greater if he had fed 1 once returned sakadagamin and after that even eating 100 sakada garments um is less than the blessings of feeding 1 non-returner anagaming and feeding a 100 non-returners is less than the merit of eating 1 arahan [Music] and the blessings of eating 1 arahan is less than the blessings of eating 1 pacca buddha is a buddha who does not spread the dhamma the teachings he practices himself and stays by himself but he shows a good example to the world and but he does not spread teach the dhamma and feeding a 100 pachika buddhas is not equivalent to feeding 1 samasan buddha what is the difference between arahan and buddha and arahan is 1 actually also used for a buddha but all buddhas are arahants but all arahants are not buddhas in the sense like all doctors are human beings but not all human beings are doctors so out of all the arahants some are buddhas and they are buddhas because they are self-enlightened buddha has no teacher in his last life he struggles by himself and attains enlightenment whereas is normally used for a disciple of a buddha having learned the dhamma from the buddha it is easier for him to become enlightened because he has the guidance given by the buddha and even feeding the sangha of monks with the buddha with the buddha at the head the creator would have would be the married of building a monastery why the greatest gift that a person can give is the giving of dhamma and when we build a monastery it helps to perpetuate the sangha of monks and when the sangha of monks is continued then the dhamma will last in the world if the sangha of manga is broken the lineage of the sangha is broken then the sasana the religion will slowly die because the dhamma would not be spread or carried on by monks so it is very important to support monks and especially bull monasteries for monks to live and to ordain others so that the lineage of the samurai can continue so that the dhamma will not die out and building monasteries is not equivalent to taking refuge you see up to this point building up the monastery is like the greatest material giving that you can give the charity the highest is building a monastery after this point it is self-cultivation self-cultivation because cultivating oneself is different from giving in giving in making offerings we are getting worldly married worldly married which brings us to a good rebirth and perhaps rebirth as a deva heavenly being or a human but it does not get us out of the round of 3 birds to get us out of the round of rebirths we have to cultivate ourselves we have to practice the aryan eightfold path namely the you can always associate to be treating sila samadhi panya moral conduct concentration and wisdom and this in chinese this uh if we cultivate ourselves now we get [Music] spiritual merit whereas if we do charity we do giving that only gives us food food is worldly blessings does not really take us out of samsara so the first step in cultivating ourselves now is to take refuge in the buddha dharma and sangha rely on the buddha dharma and sangha and greater than that is the merit of cultivating the precepts keeping the precepts later than that is the merit of practicing meta loving kindness or good will and greater than that is the thought of impermanence [Music] now this is all that is shown in this uh suta but there is another in the deegan number 5 called the where this list continues making become the thought of impermanence means intensu that means it means that 1 renounces 1 renounces becomes a renuncian or among and after that greater than the merit of renouncing is attaining the jhanas attaining the meditative absorptions and the concentrations and channels and greater than that is attaining the various higher knowledges the supernormal powers the psychic powers and up to arahanthood entertainment is the greatest uh marriedness that 1 can attain [Music] a lot of people if you don't understand like this dharma like this we think that it is very very important uh to do charity only charity is important because it helps us [Music] to get a good rebirth instead of getting a rebirth in the warfare planes but if we understand the dumb month then we should not stop there we should cultivate ourselves
